questions.q: What was the main objective of the Five-Year Plans in India?
questions.answer: The main objective of the Five-Year Plans in India was to promote rapid economic growth and industrialization, and to reduce poverty and income inequality.

questions.q: Which of the following was a key feature of the First Five-Year Plan? (A) Emphasis on heavy industry, (B) Focus on agriculture and irrigation, (C) Priority to service sector, (D) Stress on foreign investment
Fill in the blank: The ________________________ Plan is also known as the 'Mahalanobis Plan'.
Discuss the role of the Planning Commission in the development of India. What were its main functions?
questions.answer: The Planning Commission played a crucial role in the development of India by formulating Five-Year Plans. Its main functions included assessing the country's resources, determining priorities, and allocating resources to different sectors.

questions.q: True or False: The Third Five-Year Plan was successful in achieving its targets.
What were the major criticisms of the Five-Year Plans in India?
questions.answer: The major criticisms of the Five-Year Plans in India included inadequate implementation, lack of people's participation, and insufficient attention to social welfare.

questions.q: Which of the following statements is correct regarding the Eighth Five-Year Plan? (A) It was launched in 1992, (B) It focused on human development, (C) It laid emphasis on economic growth, (D) All of the above
Explain the significance of the concept of 'mixed economy' in the context of Indian planning.
questions.answer: The concept of 'mixed economy' was significant in the context of Indian planning as it allowed for the coexistence of public and private sectors, enabling the state to regulate and direct economic activity while also promoting private enterprise.

questions.q: Fill in the blank: The ________________________ sector was given top priority in the First Five-Year Plan.
What were the major differences between the First and Second Five-Year Plans?
questions.answer: The major differences between the First and Second Five-Year Plans included a shift from agriculture to industry, increased emphasis on heavy industry, and a greater role for the public sector in the Second Plan.
